System Guide + Formula Notes

This page is the web-accessible version of the repo docs. It focuses on the exact scoring equations and why each multiplier/divider exists.

Core Prediction Equation

teamContribution = (blendedAuto + blendedTeleop + blendedEndgame) * disablePenalty * foulPenalty
allianceScore = sum(teamContribution for alliance teams)

The model first estimates phase points, then scales by risk penalties. Multiplication is used because availability and foul discipline should proportionally reduce output.

Why Divide by 100

(spiderMetric / 100) * phaseMaxPoints

Spider values are 0-100 ratings. Dividing by 100 turns them into a 0-1 fraction, then multiplying by phase max points converts to estimated points.

Blend Weights

blended = local * localWeight + statbotics * (1 - localWeight)

The weights represent trust in data quality. Better local data gets higher weight; weaker local data leans toward Statbotics.

Threat Score

threat = teleop*0.40 + cycle*0.35 + reliability*0.15 + epaSignal*0.10

Teleop and cycle pace are weighted highest because they drive real match pressure. EPA is included as a smaller correction input.

Pick and Alliance Scoring

pickScore = capability*0.42 + durability*0.22 + fit*0.20 + reportScore*0.16

Capability gets the highest weight, but durability and role fit are substantial so high-risk teams do not dominate rankings.

Prediction Tactical Threat Pick Leaderboard Alliance Probabilities

Full repository file is docs.md at project root.
